checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=244000010.49 (2013-03-30) increased to 285158742.48 (2014-03-29), a 16.9% rise, while Revenues rose from 2308939067.26 (FY2013) to 2536653600.88 (FY2014), only 9.9%
evidence.location: 10-K balance sheet / income statement, FY2013 and FY2014

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=224880332.56 (2010-04-03) vs 251452830.70 (2014-03-29), +12% over a period revenue grew ~30%

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit/Revenue improved from 63.4% (FY2010: GrossProfit=1236903048.68, Revenues=1952164045.93) to 65.4% (FY2011: GrossProfit=1650147024.11, Revenues=2522721085.83) despite the inventory build

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss annual swings: 380609140.39 (FY2010) to 683396996.75 (FY2011, +79.6%) to 564369069.74 (FY2012, -17.4%) to 519074022.52 (FY2013, -8.0%) to 671166918.77 (FY2014, +29.3%), more volatile than SalesRevenueNet swings of +29.2%, -5.4%, -3.2%, +9.9% over the same periods

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3668916.92 (2012-03-31) declined to 3646558.46 (2013-03-30) and 3572030.26 (2014-03-29), while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 228870785.44 (2012-03-31) to 285158742.48 (2014-03-29) (+24.6%), lowering the allowance/AR ratio from 1.60% to 1.25%

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2009-05-28 followed by 10-K filed 2009-06-01 and a 10-K/A filed 2009-06-03
NT 10-Q filed 2006-08-10 preceding the 10-Q filed 2006-08-15; 10-Q/A filings on 2005-08-12 and 2007-02-06

LiabilitiesCurrent=411808775.20 (2013-03-30) jumped to 1493200212.53 (2013-06-29), remained near 1.5-1.6B through 2013-12-28 (1524716059.85), then fell to 1053360315.80 (2014-03-29) -- a swing not corroborated by AccountsPayableCurrent (77473130.85 to 159378560.36 over the same span) or by NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities (only -34123269.71 in the quarter of the jump)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided for Assets, AssetsCurrent, LiabilitiesCurrent, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Revenues/SalesRevenueNet, NetIncomeLoss, and cash flow statement items spanning fiscal years 2009 through 2014
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent has only three annual data points (2012-03-31, 2013-03-30, 2014-03-29), limiting granularity of reserve-trend analysis

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible misclassification or reclassification between current and non-current liabilities (or a debt/obligation recognized then reversed) not corroborated by financing cash flows or accounts payable movements, suggesting the current liabilities balance may have been temporarily overstated during 2013 (or the FY2013 year-end balance understated) rather than reflecting genuine operating activity.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities=-34123269.71 (quarter 2013-03-31 to 2013-06-29), too small to explain a >$1B increase in current liabilities

LongTermDebtCurrent=0.00 (2013-03-30) vs LongTermDebtNoncurrent=982351974.14 (2013-03-30); LongTermDebtCurrent=601550125.12 (2014-03-29) vs LongTermDebtNoncurrent=1058162061.40 (2014-03-29) -- no matching reduction in noncurrent debt to explain the earlier current-liability spike

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential channel stuffing or aggressive revenue/receivable recognition around quarter boundaries: inventory build (FY2011) and receivables spikes (Q2 FY2011) outpaced revenue growth, which could indicate pulling in sales or building inventory ahead of demand, later reversed.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=592782414.48 (2010-10-02) vs prior quarter 378396717.47 (2010-07-03), a 56.5% jump while SalesRevenueNet rose only 4.2% quarter-over-quarter (633209705.40 to 659751327.58)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reserve release / insufficient bad-debt allowance growth relative to expanding receivables base, which could modestly flatter net income by understating credit-risk provisions.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3668916.92 (2012-03-31) declined to 3646558.46 (2013-03-30) and 3572030.26 (2014-03-29), while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 228870785.44 to 285158742.48 (+24.6%) over the same span

top_signals: Unexplained ~$1.08B spike in LiabilitiesCurrent from FY2013 year-end ($411.8M) to Q1 FY2014 ($1.49B), sustained through three quarters and then falling to $1.05B by FY2014 year-end, with no corroborating movement in accounts payable, long-term debt reclassification, or financing cash flows
Inventory grew +102.7% in FY2011 versus only +29.2% revenue growth, and receivables spiked disproportionately in Q2 FY2011 relative to revenue
Allowance for doubtful accounts declined slightly in absolute terms while receivables grew ~25% over FY2012-FY2014, shrinking the reserve coverage ratio from 1.60% to 1.25%

Historical pattern of NT 10-K/NT 10-Q filings and 10-K/A and 10-Q/A amendments (2005-2009) indicating prior filing/reporting irregularities, though outside the immediate 2010-2014 core analysis window
